rulings.Case Title: CONSERVATORSHIP OF MARGARET G MCGARY
rulings.Calendar Matter: ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status Of Declaration Of Conservator Due To Death Of Conservatee {Person Only]
rulings.Rulings: Hearing. Appearance required. Issues may include (but not limited to): Conservator continues to fail to file final report. Conservator's appearance may be requested by Court. Counsel should make necessary arrangements to ensure Conservator's availability and remote appearance for this hearing. ***Until further notice, the Court strongly encourages remote appearances by video or telephone. Counsel and parties may appear by video or audio-only telephone through Zoom. For a video appearance, go to zoom.us, click "join a meeting" and input meeting ID 875 8017 7925 and password 722809. For an audio-only appearance through Zoom, call 1-669-900-6833 and key in meeting ID "875 8017 7925#", then participant ID "0#", then password "722809#". (Toll rates may apply).) Counsel and parties may appear by audio-only telephone through CourtCall. Review the Court's 6th Amended General Order which took effect on 07/21/2020 for more information. If counsel or any party appears in person, he or she should anticipate delays at the entrance to the courthouse and inside the courthouse. Masks must be worn at all times while in the courthouse and social distancing protocols will be strictly enforced, including inside the courtroom. Anyone making an in-person appearance must first familiarize themselves with the Court's current pandemic-related requirements and protocols by reviewing the Court's website at https://sfsuperiorcourt.org/.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF WILLIAM KUM-SEONG CHUN
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR SPOUSAL PROPERTY
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 10/5/20. The "Second Supplement" filed on 7/31/20 amended the petition, so it was not, in fact, a supplement. [CRC 7.3.] An amending pleading requires the same notice as the pleading it amends. [CRC 7.53(a).] The "Second Supplement" must be filed as an amendment and notice of the amendment must be served or, at the very least, a copy of the "Second Supplement" must be served with notice of the continued hearing. Alternatively, waivers of notice by all interested parties could be filed. Finally, Petitioner has not adequately addressed why the property did not pass by operation of law to the surviving joint tenant(s) other than to claim that it was the couple's understanding that all property they owned was community property.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF MICHAEL SHIU KIN NG
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR PROBATE OF WILL AND FOR LETTERS TESTAMENTARY
rulings.Rulings: Hearing. Both counsel must appear to update the Court. ***Until further notice, the Court strongly encourages remote appearances by video or telephone. Counsel and parties may appear by video or audio-only telephone through Zoom. For a video appearance, go to zoom.us, click "join a meeting" and input meeting ID 875 8017 7925 and password 722809. For an audio-only appearance through Zoom, call 1-669-900-6833 and key in meeting ID "875 8017 7925#", then participant ID "0#", then password "722809#". (Toll rates may apply).) Counsel and parties may appear by audio-only telephone through CourtCall. Review the Court's 6th Amended General Order which took effect on 07/21/2020 for more information. If counsel or any party appears in person, he or she should anticipate delays at the entrance to the courthouse and inside the courthouse. Masks must be worn at all times while in the courthouse and social distancing protocols will be strictly enforced, including inside the courtroom. Anyone making an in-person appearance must first familiarize themselves with the Court's current pandemic-related requirements and protocols by reviewing the Court's website at https://sfsuperiorcourt.org/.
